vulnerability.reason: Generated describes lack of synthetic feasibility checks, not bias toward common scaffolds and missing rare novelty.
vulnerability.reference: Researchers treat AI-generated chemical space as unbiased, reinforcing existing scaffold preferences and missing rare novelty.
vulnerability.generated: Researchers lack integrated synthetic feasibility checks, allowing chemically invalid but in silico promising structures to be accepted as leads.

impact.reason: Affected party (researchers/drug discovery) present, but damage type (wasted resources/delay) differs from reduced chemical diversity and missed opportunities.
impact.reference: Reduced chemical diversity in libraries, diminished innovation, and missed first-in-class drug opportunities.
impact.generated: Experimental resources wasted on unsynthesizable leads, delaying identification of viable drug candidates.

dose_response.reason: Generated causal arc involves missing feasibility checks leading to invalid leads, not discarding rare fragments causing bias toward common scaffolds.
dose_response.reference: Goal-aware fragment assembly discarding rare fragments skews exploration to common scaffolds, and when researchers treat this biased output as unbiased, it reduces chemical diversity and misses first-in-class opportunities.
dose_response.generated: Goal-aware fragment assembly with dynamic vocabulary optimization, through researchers exploring chemical space with fragment-based models, triggers acceptance of chemically invalid leads due to missing synthetic feasibility checks, leading to experimental resources wasted on unsynthesizable leads, delaying viable drug candidate identification.
